category_title: White students exhibiting color-blind racial ideology in response to diverse peer perspectives
scenario_title: Group research design and peer conflict over racial diversity
minimal_context: Donna is meeting privately with her instructor to discuss her recent journal entry regarding her group's progress on developing an interview guide and selecting interviewees.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's discuss your recent journal entry about your group's progress on developing the interview guide and selecting interviewees.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Yes, I wrote about some mean and terrible thoughts I was having about Charles. Like I noted, because this is a journal entry, I feel the freedom to express these thoughts.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Can you elaborate on what happened during the group discussions?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: When we were discussing interview questions, Charles contributed that he felt there was no racial diversity on our campus and depending on the age and race of the student we interviewed, they would have different answers.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What are your thoughts on his suggestions for the research?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: He got really loud and rambled on in an increasingly dominant voice about how he was Hispanic and older. I don’t understand this — we’re all different and come from different backgrounds. Race shouldn’t matter and we should see past it.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's discuss your recent journal entry about your group's progress on developing the interview guide and selecting interviewees.

STUDENT: Yes, I wrote about some mean and terrible thoughts I was having about Charles. Like I noted, because this is a journal entry, I feel the freedom to express these thoughts.

TUTOR: Can you elaborate on what happened during the group discussions?

STUDENT: When we were discussing interview questions, Charles contributed that he felt there was no racial diversity on our campus and depending on the age and race of the student we interviewed, they would have different answers.

TUTOR: What are your thoughts on his suggestions for the research?

STUDENT: He got really loud and rambled on in an increasingly dominant voice about how he was Hispanic and older. I don’t understand this — we’re all different and come from different backgrounds. Race shouldn’t matter and we should see past it.

task.content: Developing an interview guide and in selecting interviewees, designing research methodology and sampling strategy
task.learning_goal: Engage simultaneously in data collection and analysis for qualitative research

context.environment: Classroom discussion and on-line discussions with their research team of peers
context.materials: Weekly journal reflections, assigned article on campus racism
context.social_setting: Group work with a diverse peer (Charles) and private instructor-student journal dialogue

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When we were discussing interview questions Charles contributed that he felt there was no racial diversity on our campus and depending on the age and race of the student we interviewed, they would have different answers
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Rejected Charles’s insistence that students ensure a diverse sample for the group’s research and to include interview questions that explored racial dimensions; privately characterized Charles as ‘playing the race card’ while some shared their annoyance with his criticism and communication style
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Felt race was no longer a factor in shaping academic experience; experienced annoyance and 'mean and terrible thoughts' about Charles
related_quotes: He got really loud and rambled on in an increasingly dominant voice about how he was Hispanic and older ...On the discussion boards he talks about how he wants team cohesion so we can work better together yet he continually mentions his race and age ...I don’t understand this — we’re all different and come from different backgrounds ...race shouldn’t matter and we should see past it.

behavior_patterns.condition: When a diverse peer insists on attending to race and uses a non-standard communication style
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Imposing the standard verbal style they used as more legitimate and scholarly; rejecting the peer's insistence to attend to race
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Rhetoric of color-blindness shaped students’ rejection; posits race should not be (and is not) a factor in any interactions
related_quotes: race shouldn’t matter and we should see past it.

behavior_patterns.condition: When faced with the cognitive work of analysis in qualitative research
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Expressing difficulty in processing information, finding patterns, and applying it to the larger picture in private journals
related_quotes: It is much easier to regurgitate information than to process it, try to find patterns, make sense of it, and see it applied in the larger picture
